content: Among the following, the molecules /ions having same bond order are
explanation:
The molecules/ions that have the same bond order are:

Explanation

For $\mathrm{O}_2^{2-}$ (18 electrons):

Electron Configuration: $ KK \ \sigma(2s)^2 \ \sigma^{*}(2s)^2 \ \sigma(2p_z)^2\ \pi(2p_x)^2\ \pi^*(2p_y)^2\ \pi^*(2p_x)^2\ \pi^*(2p_y)^2 $

Bond Order (B.O.) calculation:

$ \text{B.O.} = \frac{\text{Number of bonding electrons} - \text{Number of antibonding electrons}}{2} = \frac{8 - 6}{2} = 1 $

For $\mathrm{B}_2$ (10 electrons):

Electron Configuration: $ KK \ \sigma(2s)^2 \ \sigma^*(2s)^2 \ \pi(2p_x)^1 \ \pi(2p_y)^1 $

Bond Order (B.O.) calculation:

$ \text{B.O.} = \frac{\text{Number of bonding electrons} - \text{Number of antibonding electrons}}{2} = \frac{4 - 2}{2} = 1 $

Thus, $\mathrm{O}_2^{2-}$ and $\mathrm{B}_2$ both have a bond order of 1, indicating they have identical bond orders.

content: Which of the following has maximum dipole moment?
explanation:
Electronegativity difference between $\mathrm{N}(3.0)$ and $\mathrm{Cl}(3.0)$ is zero and hence, $\mathrm{N}-\mathrm{Cl}$ bonds are non-polar. As a result, the overall dipole moment of $\mathrm{NCl}_3$ molecule and its direction is just the dipole moment of the lone pair of electrons.

On the other hand, $\mathrm{N}-\mathrm{Br}(3.0-2.8)$, $\mathrm{N}-\mathrm{I}(3.0-2.5)$ and $\mathrm{N}-\mathrm{H}(3.0-2.1)$ are polar and hence, contribute towards the overall dipole moment of the respective molecules. Since the EN difference is highest in case of $\mathrm{N}-\mathrm{H}$ bonds, therefore, $\mathrm{NH}_3$ has the highest dipole moment.

Hydrogen bonding is maximum in ethanol. This bonding is observed when $$\mathrm{H}$$ atom is attached to more electronegative atoms like N, F or O atom.

In ethanol, the hydrogen atom is attached directly to the electronegative element. Hence, hydrogen bonding occurs while in other given compound its not takes place.
$$\mathrm{XeOF}_4$$ has square pyramidal structure. In $$\mathrm{XeOF}_4, \mathrm{Xe}$$ is $$s p^3 d^2$$-hybridised and has 5 bond-pairs and 1 lone-pair of electrons.

content: $$\mathrm{PH}_3$$ has much lower boiling point than $$\mathrm{NH}_3$$ because :

options.content: $$\mathrm{PH}_3$$ contains covalent bonds whereas $$\mathrm{NH}_3$$ contains ionic bonds.

explanation: Boiling point of ammonia $$(\mathrm{NH}_3)$$ is much higher than phosphine $$(\mathrm{PH}_3)$$. It is due to extensive inter molecular hydrogen bonding found in ammonia.

The ortho-silicate ion or group have $$\mathrm{SiO}_4^{4-}$$ formula. It has tetrahedral shape, with one silicon atom surrounded by four oxygen atoms. In the ion, each oxygen carries a unit negative charge. The $$\mathrm{Si}-\mathrm{O}$$ bond is 162 pm long.

content: Which of the following option is correct regarding $$\mathrm{OF}$$ and $$\mathrm{F}_2$$ ?

options.content: $$\mathrm{O F}$$ is paramagnetic while $$\mathrm{F}_2$$ is diamagnetic.
$$\mathrm{OF}$$ is more stable towards dissociation into atoms than $$\mathrm{F}_2$$.
None of the above is correct.

explanation:
Species Electrons Bond order Unpaired electron
$$\mathrm{OF}$$ 17 1.5 1 Paramagnetic
$$\mathrm{F_2}$$ 18 1.0 0 Diamagnetic

There is one unpaired electron in $$(\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{F})$$, hence it is paramagnetic. Thus, statement (a) is correct. Bond order of $$\mathrm{OF}>\mathrm{F}_2$$ Thus, bond energy of $$\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{F}>\mathrm{F}-\mathrm{F}$$. Thus, statement (b) is correct.

content:
Match the species given in column I with the shape given in column II and assign the correct code.

Column I (Species) Column II
(Shape)
A. $$\mathrm{SF_4}$$ (I) Tetrahedral
B. $$\mathrm{HClO_2}$$ (II) Pyramidal
C. $$\mathrm{BrO_3^-}$$ (III) See-saw shaped
D. $$\mathrm{NH_4^+}$$ (IV)
 Angular
explanation:
Thus, the correct order is

$$\mathrm{A} \rightarrow \mathrm{III}, \mathrm{B} \rightarrow \mathrm{IV}, \mathrm{C} \rightarrow \mathrm{II}, \mathrm{D} \rightarrow \mathrm{I}.$$

content: Among the given species the isostructural pairs is
explanation:
Isostructural species are those which have the same shape and hybridisation.

$$\mathrm{NF}_3 \Rightarrow 3$$ bond pairs +1 lone pair = pyramidal shape

$$\mathrm{BF}_3 \Rightarrow 3$$ bond pairs +0 lone pair = trigonal shape

$$\mathrm{BF}_4^{-} \Rightarrow 4$$ bond pairs +0 lone pair = tetrahedral shape

$$\mathrm{NH}_4^{+} \Rightarrow 4$$ bond pairs +0 lone pair $$=$$ tetrahedral shape

$$\mathrm{BCl}_3 \Rightarrow 3$$ bond pairs +0 lone pair $$=$$ trigonal planar shape

$$\mathrm{BrCl}_3 \Rightarrow 3$$ bond pairs +2 lone pair $$=\mathrm{T}$$-shaped

$$\mathrm{NH}_3 \Rightarrow 3$$ bond pairs +1 lone pair $$=$$ pyramidal shape

$$\mathrm{NO}_3^{-} \Rightarrow 3$$ bond pairs +0 lone pair $$=$$ trigonal planar shape

content: Which of the following statements is correct?

options.content: $$s p^3$$-hybrid orbitals have equal $$s$$ and $$p$$ character
The bond angle decreases with the decrease of $$s$$ character of a hybridized orbital
Resonance decreases the stability of a molecule
Resonance is due to delocalization of sigma electrons

explanation:
The bond angle decreases with the decrease of $$s$$-character of hybridized orbital.

$$\begin{array}{lccc} \text { Hybridization } & s p^3 & s p^2 & s p \\ \text { Bond angle } & 109^{\circ} 28^{\prime} & 120^{\circ} & 180^{\circ} \end{array}$$
